skills.skill: Analyze data to select the appropriate model from among linear, quadratic, and exponential models;
skills.prompt: This topic covers analyze data to select the appropriate model from among linear, quadratic, and exponential models; for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(A)). Examine data patterns: constant rate of change = linear; U-shape/constant second differences = quadratic; constant percentage change = exponential. Key vocabulary includes: linear model, quadratic model, exponential model, scatter plot, regression. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Identify linear, quadratic, and exponential functions from graphs
skills.prompt: This topic covers identify linear, quadratic, and exponential functions from graphs for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(A)). Linear: straight line. Quadratic: parabola (U-shaped). Exponential: rapid growth/decay with horizontal asymptote. Distinguish by shape, symmetry, and behavior. Key vocabulary includes: linear, quadratic, exponential, parabola, asymptote. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Identify linear, quadratic, and exponential functions from tables
skills.prompt: This topic covers identify linear, quadratic, and exponential functions from tables for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(A)). First differences constant = linear. Second differences constant = quadratic. Consecutive ratios constant = exponential. Use equally spaced x-values. Key vocabulary includes: first differences, second differences, common ratio, linear, quadratic, exponential. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Use regression methods available through technology to write a linear function, a quadratic function, and an exponential function from a given set of data;
skills.prompt: This topic covers exponential regression for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(B)). Students use technology to find y = ab^x that best fits data. Steps: enter data, run exponential regression, interpret a and b, use for predictions. Correlation coefficient r indicates fit quality. Students compare exponential vs linear models. Vocabulary: regression, exponential model, correlation coefficient, residual, best fit, interpolation, extrapolation. Common errors: using model far outside data range, confusing with linear regression, and misinterpreting R^2.

skills.skill: Find the equation of a regression line
skills.prompt: This topic covers find the equation of a regression line for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(B)). Use technology to compute least-squares regression. Interpret slope and intercept in context. Evaluate fit using R^2. Key vocabulary includes: regression line, least squares, slope, y-intercept, R^2. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

This topic covers interpret regression lines for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(B)). Explain slope as average rate of change per unit increase in x. Y-intercept is predicted value when x = 0 (may not be meaningful). Assess reliability using R^2. Key vocabulary includes: regression line, slope, y-intercept, interpret, R^2, context. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Analyze a regression line of a data set
skills.prompt: This topic covers analyze a regression line of a data set for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(B)). Evaluate model fit using R^2 value and residual plots. Random residuals indicate good fit; patterned residuals suggest a different model type. Compare models. Key vocabulary includes: regression analysis, R^2, residual, residual plot, goodness of fit. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Predict and make decisions and critical judgments from a given set of data using linear, quadratic, and exponential models.
skills.prompt: This topic covers using regression models for prediction and decision-making for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(C)). Use equations for interpolation (reliable, within data range) and extrapolation (less reliable, beyond data). Evaluate model appropriateness critically. Key vocabulary: prediction, interpolation, extrapolation, reliability, model limitations. Common errors: trusting extrapolation too far, using poorly-fitting models, and not considering context.

This topic covers interpret regression lines for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(C)). Explain slope as average rate of change per unit increase in x. Y-intercept is predicted value when x = 0 (may not be meaningful). Assess reliability using R^2. Key vocabulary includes: regression line, slope, y-intercept, interpret, R^2, context. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.

skills.skill: Analyze a regression line of a data set
skills.prompt: This topic covers analyze a regression line of a data set for Algebra II students (TEKS 2A.8(C)). Evaluate model fit using R^2 value and residual plots. Random residuals indicate good fit; patterned residuals suggest a different model type. Compare models. Key vocabulary includes: regression analysis, R^2, residual, residual plot, goodness of fit. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify answers. Common errors include: computational mistakes, misapplication of formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning.
